bead.title: agent: close parent after pre-claim decomposition
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The pre-claim `too_large_decomposed` path creates child beads but keeps the decomposed parent open and execution-ineligible. That contradicts the lossless-decomposition lifecycle rule and risks leaving a half-owned parent while children should be ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2734-2743 logs successful pre-claim decomposition then releases the parent while explicitly documenting that the parent stays open until children close.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5248-5275 implements `applyPreClaimDecomposition` by setting `Extra["execution-eligible"] = false` instead of closing the parent after children are created.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go:73-77 asserts the old open/ineligible behavior and does not prove children remain `ReadyExecution` through the `Parent` metadata field.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update `applyPreClaimDecomposition` in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go so, after child beads are durably created, it closes the decomposed parent through the lifecycle/store API as completed by decomposition.
- Preserve durable traceability by recording `triage-decomposed` with generated child IDs in the event body or extra evidence.
- Clear parent claim metadata and heartbeat fields so a closed parent cannot remain half-owned.
- Preserve child readiness semantics: each child may have `Parent=<decomposed-id>` metadata, but generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent; child-to-child ordering deps remain allowed when explicitly generated.
- Update/rename tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go to prove the close and readiness behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change post-attempt decomposer or auto-epic decomposition behavior in this bead; those are separate children under ddx-e9542e0d.
- Do not implement historical tracker cleanup or the broader ddx-130cf9ab parent/ancestor dependency repair slice.
- Do not change intake classification semantics other than the lifecycle state applied after a successful lossless split.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e9542e0d.
Deps: No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. `applyPreClaimDecomposition` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` closes the decomposed parent through the lifecycle/store API after child creation instead of leaving it open with `execution-eligible=false`.
2. The pre-claim path still records a durable `triage-decomposed` event containing generated child IDs.
3. Parent claim metadata and heartbeat fields are cleared on the closed decomposed parent.
4. `TestIntake_TooLargeDecomposed_CreatesChildrenAndClosesParent` proves children are created, the parent is closed, `triage-decomposed` is recorded, and generated children with no explicit deps are in `ReadyExecution` despite `Parent=<decomposed-id>`.
5. `TestDecomposeDoesNotSelfDep` or equivalent asserts every generated child excludes the decomposed parent ID from `DepIDs()`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TooLargeDecomposed|DecomposeDoesNotSelfDep|parent_ancestor_in_deps' -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
